_comments.vault_output: Where the Obsidian vault is written. Set by /p2v-start-project.
_comments.layout: 'nested' (default): branch sections are folders, leaf sections are files. 'uniform': every section is its own folder note, so Obsidian's file explorer (which always lists folders before files) shows sections in ToC order.
_comments.library_index_link: 'auto' (default): if the vault lives inside an Obsidian vault (a .obsidian/ ancestor exists), the per-PDF MOC's parent links to the library index by its full Obsidian-root-relative path, so multiple libraries in one vault don't collide on a bare [[index]]. 'root': always use the bare [[index]] link (single standalone library).
_comments.sortspec: true (default): on build, scaffold a 'sortspec.md' note in the library root once (never overwritten). Its 'sorting-spec' frontmatter drives the community plugin 'Custom File Explorer sorting' (obsidian-custom-sort) to interleave files and folders in reading order (by numeric prefix), fixing Obsidian's default folders-before-files display without changing the nested on-disk layout. Scoped to this library's own subtree so it never reorders the rest of a larger Obsidian vault. View-only; requires the plugin to be installed to have any effect. Set false to skip scaffolding it.
_comments.split_depth: 0 = mirror the full ToC depth. N = only split down to ToC level N (deeper headings stay inside the note body).
_comments.pad_width: Zero-padding width for each ToC number segment (widens automatically for sibling groups > 99).
_comments.tier: 'clean' (default) or 'enriched' (adds inferred summaries/tags, Phase 2). On 'structural', body normalization (header/footer is still applied) and boilerplate/page-number stripping are skipped so raw extracted text is preserved.
_comments.min_text_chars: Below this total extracted text length, the PDF is treated as scanned and extraction stops unless ocr is true.
_comments.header: Passed to pymupdf4llm.to_markdown. true (default) keeps running page headers; false drops them at the source.
_comments.footer: Passed to pymupdf4llm.to_markdown. true (default) keeps page footers (often the page number); false drops them at the source -- the cleanest, most deterministic way to remove page numbers and running headers. Trade-off: footer=false can also drop legitimate footnotes on some PDFs, so it stays opt-in.
_comments.table_strategy: Passed to pymupdf4llm.to_markdown (signature-guarded). 'lines_strict' (default) is the library default and detects tables drawn with ruled lines. 'text' recovers tables from text positions -- use it for stat blocks that render as plain spaced text rather than ruled grids (e.g. wargame profile rows). 'lines' and null (disable detection) are also valid. The default leaves extractions byte-identical, so set 'text' per source via the 'sources' map only where it helps.
_comments.strip_page_numbers: clean/enriched tiers only. Drop *standalone* page-number lines (a whole line that is only a page number), bounded by the document's page count so a real number larger than the page count is kept. Table rows are NEVER removed by this: a single-cell numeric row may be a point-total subtotal, and an all-empty row may be a blank form field.
_comments.boilerplate_patterns: clean/enriched tiers only. List of regex strings for injected boilerplate that is NOT header/footer furniture -- e.g. a DRM ownership line stamped into the body and table cells. A line that is only boilerplate is dropped; a fragment is excised in place (and the line is dropped only if excision left nothing but furniture). The only table row removal is a row we emptied ourselves via excision. Example: ["\\*\\*This ebook belongs to .*?\\*\\*"].
_comments.sources: Optional per-source overrides, keyed by source_slug. Each value is a partial config merged over the global one once the slug is known, so a single shared config can give one PDF its own boilerplate_patterns / tier / split_depth / table_strategy without affecting the others. Note: slug_max_len is used to derive source_slug before the merge, so overriding it per-source has no effect on the slug. Example: { "necromunda-...": { "table_strategy": "text" } }.
_comments.fix_heading_spaces: Opt-in per source (off by default; set via the 'sources' map). Repairs headings whose display font omits the inter-word space glyph, so the extractor returns a glued run like 'RESOLVEHITS'. Uses PyMuPDF glyph geometry to reinsert a space on a heading line only where the inter-glyph gap exceeds heading_space_gap_ratio * font_size -- geometry only, never adds/alters a glyph, and applied only to markdown heading lines. Verify per source: fonts with loose intra-word kerning (e.g. Frostgrave's 'g') can over-split, so enable only where it helps.
_comments.heading_space_gap_ratio: Gap threshold (fraction of font size) above which fix_heading_spaces inserts a word space. Default 0.08 is conservative; lower is more aggressive and risks splitting real words.
_comments.write_images: Off by default. Set true to extract and embed PDF images (figures, diagrams, screenshots) into the manifest. Most PDFs contain only repeated decorative elements (logos, page headers) that add no signal for an AI-agent workflow; enable per source via the 'sources' map for PDFs whose figures carry real content. Can also be enabled at runtime with the --write-images CLI flag without changing this file.
_comments.reconstruct_index: Opt-in per source (off by default; set via the 'sources' map). Rebuilds a back-of-book alphabetical index section from PDF column geometry instead of pymupdf4llm's flattened text, which glues unrelated multi-column entries onto one line and scrambles the A-Z order. Re-reads the index pages column-by-column (one entry per line, true order), strips dotted leaders, and drops the giant 'INDEX' title + running header/footer. Page numbers stay plain text in the manifest; build_vault then links each to the note covering that page (with a heading anchor when the entry term matches a heading in that note). A section counts as an index when its ToC title matches index_title_pattern. Geometry-driven reformatting only -- never invents a term, page number, or ordering.
_comments.index_title_pattern: Case-insensitive regex a section's ToC title must match to be reconstructed as an index (when reconstruct_index is on). Default '^\s*index\s*$' matches a section literally titled 'Index'.
_comments.toc_title_pattern: Case-insensitive regex matching the heading of the document's printed table-of-contents page, used by --fallback toc to locate that page automatically. The default covers common English and European terms (contents, table of contents, toc, index, inhoudstafel, inhoudspgave, inhaltsverzeichnis, table des matières, índice, contenido). Override per source when the ToC page has an unusual title.
_comments.toc_hierarchy_mode: 'auto' (default): pick numbered-prefix inference when ≥ 60% of ToC rows carry an outline prefix (e.g. '1', '1.1', 'A.2'); otherwise use indentation-band inference. 'indent': always use x0 indentation bands to derive level. 'numbered': always use outline prefixes. Override per source via the 'sources' map.
